description_long: This object consists of four parallel rows of rounded stones, each stone varying slightly in size, shape, and coloration. The stones are predominantly light gray and off-white, with some displaying subtle beige, tan, or darker gray marbling and speckling. The arrangement is orderly, with each row containing stones of similar size, suggesting intentional placement, possibly as a barrier or demarcation. The surfaces of the stones appear naturally weathered, with a matte, slightly rough texture and no visible artificial carvings or markings. The stones are closely packed but not cemented together, allowing for small gaps between them. This configuration is typical for use as a low obstacle or boundary in urban parks, gardens, or along pathways.
description: A set of four parallel rows of rounded, weathered stones in varying shades of gray and off-white, arranged as a low barrier or boundary.
description_view_0: Front view showing the orderly arrangement of four rows of stones, with the color gradient from darker grays on the left to lighter stones on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the rows and the rounded, irregular shapes of the stones, with more visible surface texture and marbling.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the stones, emphasizing the lighter tones and the natural, unpolished surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arrangement from the other end, with the darker stones now on the right and the lighter stones on the left.
attribute_placement: arranged in parallel rows on the ground as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Natural marbling and speckling with irregular, organic patterns; no artificial carvings or regularity.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces are matte and slightly rough, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or grass, often used to demarcate paths or borders in landscaped urban environments.
